english: For the third-person singular of 'wonen', use the stem 'woon' plus -t: woont.
notes: The handwritten conjugation table contains 'woond'; this is treated as a correction candidate.

english: The stem of 'sporten' is already 'sport', so only one final t is written.
notes: Visible in the question-formation notes and included as a likely learner correction.

english: Roept is from roepen and normally means calls or shouts. Use schreeuwt for screams.
notes: The note glossed roept too strongly as screams; corrected for practical usage.

correct: jarige = birthday person; jarig = having one's birthday
english: Young is jong. Jarig relates to birthdays, and de jarige is the person whose birthday it is.
notes: The handwriting near jarig/jarige could mislead the app, so the normalized correction is explicit.

english: For going to an event such as a birthday party, use naar. Op appears in other fixed time/event phrases, but naar je verjaardag is the natural beginner phrase here.
notes: Recorded as a supported note improvement, not as a new canonical expression because naar was not the focus of the lesson.

english: The fixed expression is Het maakt me niet uit, meaning I don't mind or it doesn't matter to me.
notes: The first line appears to preserve an earlier misspelling of the known expression.
